title: Tanacetum potentilloides
intro: Tanacetum potentilloides, commonly known as Arctic tansy or potent tansy, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the aster family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities, as well as its ability to soothe digestive issues and alleviate symptoms of colds and fever. Bioactive constituents of Tanacetum potentilloides include sesquiterpene lactones, essential oils, and flavonoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, although its use is largely limited to regional and folk applications, and more research is needed to fully understand its potential uses and benefits.
taxonomy: Tanacetum potentilloides is classified within the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and morphology, which resemble those of other members in the Asteraceae family. The genus Tanacetum is further divided into several species, each with distinct features. Tanacetum potentilloides, in particular, is a member of the genus Tanacetum and is closely related to other species within the same genus. Its classification is based on a combination of morphological and molecular characteristics.
